pattern: Since sorting a substring only rearranges values that were already going to sit in that index range, comparing the string against its own fully-sorted target locates exactly the span that still needs fixing -- and whether that span touches both ends decides if one operation is enough, versus needing to fix the minimum and maximum characters into place from either side first.
hints: Compare the string to its sorted form and find the first and last index where they disagree -- that is the only span an operation would ever need to touch.

If that span does not cover the entire string (it starts after index 0, or ends before the last index), one sort of a substring covering just that span finishes the job; if it covers everything, one operation is impossible because the whole string can't be selected.

When one operation cannot work, check whether the overall minimum character shows up anywhere before the very last position, or the overall maximum character shows up anywhere after the very first position -- either lets two operations pin one extreme in place and then finish the rest; if neither holds, three operations are always enough.
A string of length 2 that isn't already sorted can never be fixed at all, since the only non-trivial substring you're allowed to pick is the whole string.

edge_inputs.description: one operation suffices, mismatch span doesn't touch the left end
mismatch spans the whole string but the minimum character is reachable from a proper prefix
length 2, not sorted -- the only substring big enough to fix it is disallowed
reverse-sorted, minimum stuck at the very end and maximum stuck at the very front -- needs all three operations
all identical characters, already sorted regardless of length
only the last two characters are swapped -- smallest possible non-trivial fix
long uniform run with a single misplaced character deep inside

stress.description: many random short strings over a small alphabet, exercising every branch (0/1/2/3/-1) via brute-forceable lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(2, size))
    alphabet = 'abcd'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: large random string exercising the linear scan at scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    alphabet = 'abcdefghij'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
